		NPNExtension implements next_protocol_negotiation (Not IANA assigned)

		
			NextProtos []string
		
			(*NPNExtension) Len() int
			(*NPNExtension) Read(b []byte) (int, error)
			
				draft-agl-tls-nextprotoneg-04:
				The "extension_data" field of a "next_protocol_negotiation" extension
				in a "ClientHello" MUST be empty.

			
				Write is a no-op for NPNExtension. NextProtos are not included in the
				ClientHello.

			
			(*NPNExtension) writeToUConn(uc *UConn) error
		
			*NPNExtension : TLSExtension
			*NPNExtension : TLSExtensionJSON
			*NPNExtension : TLSExtensionWriter
			*NPNExtension : encoding/json.Unmarshaler
			*NPNExtension : internal/bisect.Writer
			*NPNExtension : io.Reader
			*NPNExtension : io.ReadWriter
			*NPNExtension : io.Writer
			
			*NPNExtension : transcriptHash
			*NPNExtension : crypto/tls.transcriptHash
			*NPNExtension : net/http.http2pipeBuffer


	

		
			( PaddingTransportParameter) ID() uint64
			( PaddingTransportParameter) Value() []byte
		
			 PaddingTransportParameter : TransportParameter


	

		
			
				// this will be used to compute the binder when hello message is ready

			Binders [][]byte
			EarlySecret []byte
			Identities []PskIdentity
			Session *SessionState
		
			func (*FakePreSharedKeyExtension).GetPreSharedKeyCommon() PreSharedKeyCommon
			func PreSharedKeyExtension.GetPreSharedKeyCommon() PreSharedKeyCommon
			func (*UnimplementedPreSharedKeyExtension).GetPreSharedKeyCommon() PreSharedKeyCommon
			func (*UtlsPreSharedKeyExtension).GetPreSharedKeyCommon() PreSharedKeyCommon


	
		The lifecycle of a PreSharedKeyExtension:
		
		Creation Phase:
		  - The extension is created.
		
		Write Phase:
		
		  - [writeToUConn() called]:
		
		    > - During this phase, it is important to note that implementations should not write any session data to the UConn (Underlying Connection) as the session is not yet loaded. The session context is not active at this point.
		
		Initialization Phase:
		
		  - [IsInitialized() called]:
		
		    If IsInitialized() returns true
		
		    > - GetPreSharedKeyCommon() will be called subsequently and the PSK states in handshake/clientHello will be fully initialized.
		
		    If IsInitialized() returns false:
		
		    > - [conn.loadSession() called]:
		
		    >> - Once the session is available:
		
		    >>> - [InitializeByUtls() called]:
		
		    >>>> - The InitializeByUtls() method is invoked to initialize the extension based on the loaded session data.
		
		    >>>> - This step prepares the extension for further processing.
		
		Marshal Phase:
		
		  - [Len() called], [Read() called]:
		
		    > - Implementations should marshal the extension into bytes, using placeholder binders to maintain the correct length.
		
		Binders Preparation Phase:
		
		  - [PatchBuiltHello(hello) called]:
		
		    > - The client hello is already marshaled in the "hello.Raw" format.
		
		    > - Implementations are expected to update the binders within the marshaled client hello.
		
		  - [GetPreSharedKeyCommon() called]:
		
		    > - Implementations should gather and provide the final pre-shared key (PSK) related data.
		
		    > - This data will be incorporated into both the clientHello and HandshakeState, ensuring that the PSK-related information is properly set and ready for the handshake process.

		
			
				GetPreSharedKeyCommon retrieves the final PreSharedKey-related states as defined in PreSharedKeyCommon.

			
				InitializeByUtls is invoked when IsInitialized() returns false.
				It initializes the extension using a real and valid TLS 1.3 session.

			
				IsInitialized returns a boolean indicating whether the extension has been initialized.
				If false is returned, utls will initialize the extension.

			
				// includes header

			
				PatchBuiltHello is called once the hello message is fully applied and marshaled.
				Its purpose is to update the binders of PSK (Pre-Shared Key) identities.

			
				Read reads up to len(p) bytes into p.
				It returns the number of bytes read (0 <= n <= len(p)) and any error encountered.
				// implements io.Reader

			( PreSharedKeyExtension) SetOmitEmptyPsk(val bool)
			
			
				// this works like a type guard

			( PreSharedKeyExtension) writeToUConn(*UConn) error
		
			*FakePreSharedKeyExtension
			*UnimplementedPreSharedKeyExtension
			*UtlsPreSharedKeyExtension
		
			 PreSharedKeyExtension : Initializable
			 PreSharedKeyExtension : TLSExtension
			 PreSharedKeyExtension : io.Reader
		
			func (*UConn).SetPskExtension(pskExt PreSharedKeyExtension) error


	
		PRNGSeed is a PRNG seed.

		
			func NewPRNGSeed() (*PRNGSeed, error)
			
			func newSaltedPRNGSeed(seed *PRNGSeed, salt string) (*PRNGSeed, error)
		
			
			func newPRNGWithSaltedSeed(seed *PRNGSeed, salt string) (*prng, error)
			func newPRNGWithSeed(seed *PRNGSeed) (*prng, error)
			func newSaltedPRNGSeed(seed *PRNGSeed, salt string) (*PRNGSeed, error)


	

		
			( PskIdentities) ToPrivate() []pskIdentity


	
		TLS 1.3 PSK Identity. Can be a Session Ticket, or a reference to a saved
		session. See RFC 8446, Section 4.2.11.

		
			Label []byte
			ObfuscatedTicketAge uint32
		
			func (*FakePreSharedKeyExtension).InitializeByUtls(session *SessionState, earlySecret []byte, binderKey []byte, identities []PskIdentity)
			func PreSharedKeyExtension.InitializeByUtls(session *SessionState, earlySecret []byte, binderKey []byte, identities []PskIdentity)
			func (*UnimplementedPreSharedKeyExtension).InitializeByUtls(session *SessionState, earlySecret []byte, binderKey []byte, identities []PskIdentity)
			func (*UtlsPreSharedKeyExtension).InitializeByUtls(session *SessionState, earlySecret []byte, binderKey []byte, identities []PskIdentity)
			
			func pskExtLen(identities []PskIdentity, binders [][]byte) int
			func readPskIntoBytes(b []byte, identities []PskIdentity, binders [][]byte) (int, error)


	
		PSKKeyExchangeModesExtension implements psk_key_exchange_modes (45).

		
			Modes []uint8

			(*PSKKeyExchangeModesExtension) Read(b []byte) (int, error)
			(*PSKKeyExchangeModesExtension) UnmarshalJSON(b []byte) error

		A CipherSuite is a specific combination of key agreement, cipher and MAC
		function. All cipher suites currently assume RSA key agreement.

		
			Aead func(key, fixedNonce []byte) aead
			Cipher func(key, iv []byte, isRead bool) interface{}
			
				flags is a bitmask of the suite* values, above.

			Id uint16
			IvLen int
			Ka func(version uint16) keyAgreement
			
				the lengths, in bytes, of the key material needed for each component.

			Mac func(macKey []byte) hash.Hash
			MacLen int
		
			
			(*PubCipherSuite) getPrivatePtr() *cipherSuite


	

		
			Aead func(key, fixedNonce []byte) aead
			Hash crypto.Hash
			Id uint16
			KeyLen int
		
			
			(*PubCipherSuiteTLS13) toPrivate() *cipherSuiteTLS13


	
		ClientHandshakeState includes both TLS 1.3-only and TLS 1.2-only states,
		only one of them will be used, depending on negotiated version.
		
		ClientHandshakeState will be converted into and from either
		  - clientHandshakeState      (TLS 1.2)
		  - clientHandshakeStateTLS13 (TLS 1.3)
		
		uTLS will call .handshake() on one of these private internal states,
		to perform TLS handshake using standard crypto/tls implementation.

		
			C *Conn
			Hello *PubClientHelloMsg
			MasterSecret []byte
			ServerHello *PubServerHelloMsg
			Session *SessionState
			State12 TLS12OnlyState
			State13 TLS13OnlyState
			
			uconn *UConn
		
			
			(*PubClientHandshakeState) toPrivate12() *clientHandshakeState
			(*PubClientHandshakeState) toPrivate13() *clientHandshakeStateTLS13


	

		
			AlpnProtocols []string
			CipherSuites []uint16
			CompressionMethods []uint8
			Cookie []byte
			EarlyData bool
			
				// [uTLS] actually implemented due to its prevalence

			KeyShares []KeyShare
			NextProtoNeg bool
			OcspStapling bool
			PskBinders [][]byte
			PskIdentities []PskIdentity
			PskModes []uint8
			QuicTransportParameters []byte
			Random []byte
			
				// renamed to clientHelloMsg.original in crypto/tls

			Scts bool
			SecureRenegotiation []byte
			SecureRenegotiationSupported bool
			ServerName string
			SessionId []byte
			SessionTicket []uint8
			SupportedCurves []CurveID
			SupportedPoints []uint8
			SupportedSignatureAlgorithms []SignatureScheme
			
				1.3

			SupportedVersions []uint16
			TicketSupported bool
			Vers uint16
			
			
				// todo: further optimize to reduce clientHelloMsg construction

			encryptedClientHello []byte
		
			
				Marshal allows external code to convert a ClientHello object back into
				raw bytes.

			
			(*PubClientHelloMsg) getCachedPrivatePtr() *clientHelloMsg
			(*PubClientHelloMsg) getPrivatePtr() *clientHelloMsg
		
			func UnmarshalClientHello(data []byte) *PubClientHelloMsg
		
			func (*FakePreSharedKeyExtension).PatchBuiltHello(*PubClientHelloMsg) error
			func PreSharedKeyExtension.PatchBuiltHello(hello *PubClientHelloMsg) error
			func (*UnimplementedPreSharedKeyExtension).PatchBuiltHello(hello *PubClientHelloMsg) error
			func (*UtlsPreSharedKeyExtension).PatchBuiltHello(hello *PubClientHelloMsg) error


	

		
			AlpnProtocol string
			CipherSuite uint16
			CompressionMethod uint8
			
				// HelloRetryRequest extension

			ExtendedMasterSecret bool
			NextProtoNeg bool
			NextProtos []string
			OcspStapling bool
			Random []byte
			
				// renamed to serverHelloMsg.original in crypto/tls

			Scts [][]byte
			SecureRenegotiation []byte
			SecureRenegotiationSupported bool
			
				// HelloRetryRequest extension

			SelectedIdentity uint16
			SelectedIdentityPresent bool
			ServerShare KeyShare
			SessionId []byte
			
				1.3

			
				// used by go tls to determine whether to add the session ticket ext

			Vers uint16
		
			
			(*PubServerHelloMsg) getPrivatePtr() *serverHelloMsg


	
		A QUICConfig configures a [QUICConn].

		
			
				EnableSessionEvents may be set to true to enable the
				[QUICStoreSession] and [QUICResumeSession] events for client connections.
				When this event is enabled, sessions are not automatically
				stored in the client session cache.
				The application should use [QUICConn.StoreSession] to store sessions.

			TLSConfig *Config
		
			func QUICClient(config *QUICConfig) *QUICConn
			func QUICServer(config *QUICConfig) *QUICConn
			func UQUICClient(config *QUICConfig, clientHelloID ClientHelloID) *UQUICConn
			
			func newQUICConn(conn *Conn, config *QUICConfig) *QUICConn


	
		A QUICConn represents a connection which uses a QUIC implementation as the underlying
		transport as described in RFC 9001.
		
		Methods of QUICConn are not safe for concurrent use.

		
			
			conn *Conn
			sessionTicketSent bool
		
			
				Close closes the connection and stops any in-progress handshake.

			
				ConnectionState returns basic TLS details about the connection.

			
				HandleData handles handshake bytes received from the peer.
				It may produce connection events, which may be read with [QUICConn.NextEvent].

			
				NextEvent returns the next event occurring on the connection.
				It returns an event with a Kind of [QUICNoEvent] when no events are available.

			
				SendSessionTicket sends a session ticket to the client.
				It produces connection events, which may be read with [QUICConn.NextEvent].
				Currently, it can only be called once.

			
				SetTransportParameters sets the transport parameters to send to the peer.
				
				Server connections may delay setting the transport parameters until after
				receiving the client's transport parameters. See [QUICTransportParametersRequired].

			
				Start starts the client or server handshake protocol.
				It may produce connection events, which may be read with [QUICConn.NextEvent].
				
				Start must be called at most once.

			
				StoreSession stores a session previously received in a QUICStoreSession event
				in the ClientSessionCache.
				The application may process additional events or modify the SessionState
				before storing the session.

		
			*QUICConn : io.Closer
		
			func QUICClient(config *QUICConfig) *QUICConn
			func QUICServer(config *QUICConfig) *QUICConn
			
			func newQUICConn(conn *Conn, config *QUICConfig) *QUICConn


	
		QUICEncryptionLevel represents a QUIC encryption level used to transmit
		handshake messages.

		
			( QUICEncryptionLevel) String() string
		
			 QUICEncryptionLevel : fmt.Stringer
			
			 QUICEncryptionLevel : context.stringer
			 QUICEncryptionLevel : runtime.stringer
		
			func (*QUICConn).HandleData(level QUICEncryptionLevel, data []byte) error
			func (*UConn).QUICSetReadSecret(level QUICEncryptionLevel, suite uint16, secret []byte)
			func (*UConn).QUICSetWriteSecret(level QUICEncryptionLevel, suite uint16, secret []byte)
			func (*UQUICConn).HandleData(level QUICEncryptionLevel, data []byte) error
			
			func (*Conn).quicSetReadSecret(level QUICEncryptionLevel, suite uint16, secret []byte)
			func (*Conn).quicSetWriteSecret(level QUICEncryptionLevel, suite uint16, secret []byte)
			func (*Conn).quicWriteCryptoData(level QUICEncryptionLevel, data []byte)
		
			const QUICEncryptionLevelApplication
			const QUICEncryptionLevelEarly
			const QUICEncryptionLevelHandshake
			const QUICEncryptionLevelInitial


	
		A QUICEvent is an event occurring on a QUIC connection.
		
		The type of event is specified by the Kind field.
		The contents of the other fields are kind-specific.

		
			
				Set for QUICTransportParameters, QUICSetReadSecret, QUICSetWriteSecret, and QUICWriteData.
				The contents are owned by crypto/tls, and are valid until the next NextEvent call.

			Kind QUICEventKind
			
				Set for QUICSetReadSecret, QUICSetWriteSecret, and QUICWriteData.

			
				Set for QUICResumeSession and QUICStoreSession.

			
				Set for QUICSetReadSecret and QUICSetWriteSecret.

		
			func (*QUICConn).NextEvent() QUICEvent
			func (*UQUICConn).NextEvent() QUICEvent


	
		A QUICEventKind is a type of operation on a QUIC connection.

		
			const QUICHandshakeDone
			const QUICNoEvent
			const QUICRejectedEarlyData
			const QUICResumeSession
			const QUICSetReadSecret
			const QUICSetWriteSecret
			const QUICStoreSession
			const QUICTransportParameters
			const QUICTransportParametersRequired
			const QUICWriteData


	

		
			
				EarlyData specifies whether the ticket may be used for 0-RTT.

			Extra [][]byte
		
			func (*QUICConn).SendSessionTicket(opts QUICSessionTicketOptions) error
			func (*UQUICConn).SendSessionTicket(opts QUICSessionTicketOptions) error


	
		QUICTransportParametersExtension implements quic_transport_parameters (57).
		
		Currently, it works as a fake extension and does not support parsing, since
		the QUICConn provided by this package does not really understand these
		parameters.

		
			TransportParameters TransportParameters
			
			
				// TransportParameters will be marshaled into this slice

		
			(*QUICTransportParametersExtension) Len() int
			(*QUICTransportParametersExtension) Read(b []byte) (int, error)
			
			(*QUICTransportParametersExtension) writeToUConn(*UConn) error
		
			*QUICTransportParametersExtension : TLSExtension
			*QUICTransportParametersExtension : io.Reader


	
		RecordHeaderError is returned when a TLS record header is invalid.

		
			
				Conn provides the underlying net.Conn in the case that a client
				sent an initial handshake that didn't look like TLS.
				It is nil if there's already been a handshake or a TLS alert has
				been written to the connection.

			
				Msg contains a human readable string that describes the error.

			
				RecordHeader contains the five bytes of TLS record header that
				triggered the error.

		
			( RecordHeaderError) Error() string
		
			 RecordHeaderError : error
		
			
			func (*Conn).newRecordHeaderError(conn net.Conn, msg string) (err RecordHeaderError)


	
		RenegotiationInfoExtension implements renegotiation_info (65281)

		
			
				RenegotiatedConnection is not yet properly handled, now we
				are just copying it to the client hello.
				
				If this is the initial handshake for a connection, then the
				"renegotiated_connection" field is of zero length in both the
				ClientHello and the ServerHello.

			
				Renegotiation field limits how many times client will perform renegotiation: no limit, once, or never.
				The extension still will be sent, even if Renegotiation is set to RenegotiateNever.
				// [UTLS] added for internal use only

		
			(*RenegotiationInfoExtension) Len() int
			(*RenegotiationInfoExtension) Read(b []byte) (int, error)
			(*RenegotiationInfoExtension) UnmarshalJSON(_ []byte) error
			(*RenegotiationInfoExtension) Write(b []byte) (int, error)
			
			(*RenegotiationInfoExtension) writeToUConn(uc *UConn) error
		
			*RenegotiationInfoExtension : TLSExtension
			*RenegotiationInfoExtension : TLSExtensionJSON
			*RenegotiationInfoExtension : TLSExtensionWriter
			*RenegotiationInfoExtension : encoding/json.Unmarshaler
			*RenegotiationInfoExtension : internal/bisect.Writer
			*RenegotiationInfoExtension : io.Reader
			*RenegotiationInfoExtension : io.ReadWriter
			*RenegotiationInfoExtension : io.Writer
			
			*RenegotiationInfoExtension : transcriptHash
			*RenegotiationInfoExtension : crypto/tls.transcriptHash
			*RenegotiationInfoExtension : net/http.http2pipeBuffer


	
		RenegotiationSupport enumerates the different levels of support for TLS
		renegotiation. TLS renegotiation is the act of performing subsequent
		handshakes on a connection after the first. This significantly complicates
		the state machine and has been the source of numerous, subtle security
		issues. Initiating a renegotiation is not supported, but support for
		accepting renegotiation requests may be enabled.
		
		Even when enabled, the server may not change its identity between handshakes
		(i.e. the leaf certificate must be the same). Additionally, concurrent
		handshake and application data flow is not permitted so renegotiation can
		only be used with protocols that synchronise with the renegotiation, such as
		HTTPS.
		
		Renegotiation is not defined in TLS 1.3.

		
			const RenegotiateFreelyAsClient
			const RenegotiateNever
			const RenegotiateOnceAsClient


	

		
			HelloIDMu sync.Mutex
			HelloIDs []ClientHelloID
			TcpDialTimeout time.Duration
			TlsHandshakeTimeout time.Duration
			WorkingHelloID *ClientHelloID
			
			r *prng
		
			
				Dial attempts to establish connection to given address using different HelloIDs.
				If a working HelloID is found, it is used again for subsequent Dials.
				If tcp connection fails or all HelloIDs are tried, returns with last error.
				
				Usage examples:
				   Dial("tcp4", "google.com:443", "google.com")
				   Dial("tcp", "10.23.144.22:443", "mywebserver.org")

		
			func NewRoller() (*Roller, error)


	
		SCTExtension implements signed_certificate_timestamp (18)

		
			(*SCTExtension) Len() int
			(*SCTExtension) Read(b []byte) (int, error)
			(*SCTExtension) UnmarshalJSON(_ []byte) error
			(*SCTExtension) Write(_ []byte) (int, error)
			
			(*SCTExtension) writeToUConn(uc *UConn) error
		
			*SCTExtension : TLSExtension
			*SCTExtension : TLSExtensionJSON
			*SCTExtension : TLSExtensionWriter
			*SCTExtension : encoding/json.Unmarshaler
			*SCTExtension : internal/bisect.Writer
			*SCTExtension : io.Reader
			*SCTExtension : io.ReadWriter
			*SCTExtension : io.Writer
			
			*SCTExtension : transcriptHash
			*SCTExtension : crypto/tls.transcriptHash
			*SCTExtension : net/http.http2pipeBuffer


	
		A SessionState is a resumable session.

		
			
				EarlyData indicates whether the ticket can be used for 0-RTT in a QUIC
				connection. The application may set this to false if it is true to
				decline to offer 0-RTT even if supported.

			
				Extra is ignored by crypto/tls, but is encoded by [SessionState.Bytes]
				and parsed by [ParseSessionState].
				
				This allows [Config.UnwrapSession]/[Config.WrapSession] and
				[ClientSessionCache] implementations to store and retrieve additional
				data alongside this session.
				
				To allow different layers in a protocol stack to share this field,
				applications must only append to it, not replace it, and must use entries
				that can be recognized even if out of order (for example, by starting
				with an id and version prefix).

			
			activeCertHandles []*activeCert
			ageAdd uint32
			
				// only set if EarlyData is true

			cipherSuite uint16
			
				createdAt is the generation time of the secret on the sever (which for
				TLS 1.0–1.2 might be earlier than the current session) and the time at
				which the ticket was received on the client.
				// seconds since UNIX epoch

			extMasterSecret bool
			isClient bool
			ocspResponse []byte
			peerCertificates []*x509.Certificate
			scts [][]byte
			
				// master secret for TLS 1.2, or the PSK for TLS 1.3

			ticket []byte
			
				Client-side TLS 1.3-only fields.
				// seconds since UNIX epoch

			verifiedChains [][]*x509.Certificate
			version uint16
		
			
				Bytes encodes the session, including any private fields, so that it can be
				parsed by [ParseSessionState]. The encoding contains secret values critical
				to the security of future and possibly past sessions.
				
				The specific encoding should be considered opaque and may change incompatibly
				between Go versions.

		
			func ParseSessionState(data []byte) (*SessionState, error)
			func (*ClientSessionState).ResumptionState() (ticket []byte, state *SessionState, err error)
			func (*Config).DecryptTicket(identity []byte, cs ConnectionState) (*SessionState, error)
			func ISessionTicketExtension.GetSession() *SessionState
			func (*SessionTicketExtension).GetSession() *SessionState
			
			func (*Conn).loadSession(hello *clientHelloMsg) (session *SessionState, earlySecret *tls13.EarlySecret, binderKey []byte, err error)
			func (*Conn).sessionState() *SessionState
		
			func NewResumptionState(ticket []byte, state *SessionState) (*ClientSessionState, error)
			func (*Config).EncryptTicket(cs ConnectionState, ss *SessionState) ([]byte, error)
			func (*FakePreSharedKeyExtension).InitializeByUtls(session *SessionState, earlySecret []byte, binderKey []byte, identities []PskIdentity)
			func ISessionTicketExtension.InitializeByUtls(session *SessionState, ticket []byte)
			func PreSharedKeyExtension.InitializeByUtls(session *SessionState, earlySecret []byte, binderKey []byte, identities []PskIdentity)
			func (*QUICConn).StoreSession(session *SessionState) error
			func (*SessionTicketExtension).InitializeByUtls(session *SessionState, ticket []byte)
			func (*UnimplementedPreSharedKeyExtension).InitializeByUtls(session *SessionState, earlySecret []byte, binderKey []byte, identities []PskIdentity)
			func (*UtlsPreSharedKeyExtension).InitializeByUtls(session *SessionState, earlySecret []byte, binderKey []byte, identities []PskIdentity)
			
			func (*Conn).quicResumeSession(session *SessionState) error
			func (*Conn).quicStoreSession(session *SessionState)

		SignatureScheme identifies a signature algorithm supported by TLS. See
		RFC 8446, Section 4.2.3.

			 SignatureScheme : context.stringer
			 SignatureScheme : runtime.stringer
		
			
			func selectSignatureScheme(vers uint16, c *Certificate, peerAlgs []SignatureScheme) (SignatureScheme, error)
			func signatureSchemesForCertificate(version uint16, cert *Certificate) []SignatureScheme
			func supportedSignatureAlgorithms() []SignatureScheme
		
			
			func isSupportedSignatureAlgorithm(sigAlg SignatureScheme, supportedSignatureAlgorithms []SignatureScheme) bool
			func isSupportedSignatureAlgorithm(sigAlg SignatureScheme, supportedSignatureAlgorithms []SignatureScheme) bool
			func selectSignatureScheme(vers uint16, c *Certificate, peerAlgs []SignatureScheme) (SignatureScheme, error)
			func typeAndHashFromSignatureScheme(signatureAlgorithm SignatureScheme) (sigType uint8, hash crypto.Hash, err error)

		TicketKey is the internal representation of a session ticket key.

		
			AesKey [16]byte
			
				created is the time at which this ticket key was created. See Config.ticketKeys.

			HmacKey [16]byte
		
			( TicketKey) ToPrivate() ticketKey
		
			func TicketKeyFromBytes(b [32]byte) TicketKey


	

		
			( TicketKeys) ToPrivate() []ticketKey


	
		TLS 1.2 and before only

		
			FinishedHash FinishedHash
			Suite PubCipherSuite


	
		TLS 1.3 only

		
			BinderKey []byte
			CertReq *CertificateRequestMsgTLS13
			EarlySecret []byte
			
				Deprecated: Use KeyShareKeys instead. KeyShareKeys will take precedence if both are set.
				Support may be removed in the future.

			
				Deprecated: Use KeyShareKeys instead. This variable is no longer used.
				Will be removed in the future.

			KeyShareKeys *KeySharePrivateKeys
			
				Deprecated: Use KeyShareKeys instead. This variable is no longer used.
				Will be removed in the future.

			SentDummyCCS bool
			Suite *PubCipherSuiteTLS13
			
				// client_application_traffic_secret_0

			Transcript hash.Hash
			
				// don't set this field when building client hello

		
			
			(*TLS13OnlyState) private13KeyShareKeys() *keySharePrivateKeys


	

		
			
				// includes header

			
				Read reads up to len(p) bytes into p.
				It returns the number of bytes read (0 <= n <= len(p)) and any error encountered.
				// implements io.Reader

			
			( TLSExtension) writeToUConn(*UConn) error

			 TLSExtension : io.Reader

			func genericExtension(id uint16, name string) TLSExtension

			func (*UConn).SetTLSVers(minTLSVers, maxTLSVers uint16, specExtensions []TLSExtension) error


	

		
			
				// includes header

			
				Read reads up to len(p) bytes into p.
				It returns the number of bytes read (0 <= n <= len(p)) and any error encountered.
				// implements io.Reader

			
				UnmarshalJSON unmarshals the JSON-encoded data into the extension.

			
			( TLSExtensionJSON) writeToUConn(*UConn) error
		
			*ALPNExtension
			*ApplicationSettingsExtension
			*ApplicationSettingsExtensionNew

			 TLSExtensionJSON : TLSExtension
			 TLSExtensionJSON : encoding/json.Unmarshaler

				// if set, unknown extensions will be added as GenericExtension, without recovering ext payload

			
				// if set, PSK extension will be real PSK extension, otherwise it will be fake PSK extension

			
			extensions []TLSExtensionJSON
		
			(*TLSExtensionsJSONUnmarshaler) Extensions() []TLSExtension
			(*TLSExtensionsJSONUnmarshaler) UnmarshalJSON(jsonStr []byte) error
		
			*TLSExtensionsJSONUnmarshaler : encoding/json.Unmarshaler


	
		TLSExtensionWriter is an interface allowing a TLS extension to be
		auto-constucted/recovered by reading in a byte stream.

		
			
				// includes header

			
				Read reads up to len(p) bytes into p.
				It returns the number of bytes read (0 <= n <= len(p)) and any error encountered.
				// implements io.Reader

			
				Write writes the extension data as a byte slice, up to len(b) bytes from b.
				It returns the number of bytes written (0 <= n <= len(b)) and any error encountered.
				
				The implementation MUST NOT silently drop data if consumed less than len(b) bytes,
				instead, it MUST return an error.

			
			( TLSExtensionWriter) writeToUConn(*UConn) error
		
			*ALPNExtension
			*ApplicationSettingsExtension
			*ApplicationSettingsExtensionNew
			*ExtendedMasterSecretExtension
			*FakeChannelIDExtension
			*FakeDelegatedCredentialsExtension
			*FakePreSharedKeyExtension
			*FakeRecordSizeLimitExtension
			*FakeTokenBindingExtension
			*GREASEEncryptedClientHelloExtension
			*KeyShareExtension
			*NPNExtension
			*PSKKeyExchangeModesExtension
			*RenegotiationInfoExtension
			*SCTExtension
			*SessionTicketExtension
			*SignatureAlgorithmsCertExtension
			*SignatureAlgorithmsExtension
			*SNIExtension
			*StatusRequestExtension
			*StatusRequestV2Extension
			*SupportedCurvesExtension
			*SupportedPointsExtension
			*SupportedVersionsExtension
			*UtlsCompressCertExtension
			*UtlsGREASEExtension
			*UtlsPaddingExtension
			*UtlsPreSharedKeyExtension
		
			 TLSExtensionWriter : TLSExtension
			 TLSExtensionWriter : internal/bisect.Writer
			 TLSExtensionWriter : io.Reader
			 TLSExtensionWriter : io.ReadWriter
			 TLSExtensionWriter : io.Writer
			
			 TLSExtensionWriter : transcriptHash
			 TLSExtensionWriter : crypto/tls.transcriptHash
			 TLSExtensionWriter : net/http.http2pipeBuffer


	
		TransportParameter represents a QUIC transport parameter.
		
		Caller will write the following to the wire:
		
			var b []byte
			b = quicvarint.Append(b, ID())
			b = quicvarint.Append(b, len(Value()))
			b = append(b, Value())
		
		Therefore Value() should return the exact bytes to be written to the wire AFTER the length field,
		i.e., the bytes MAY be a Variable Length Integer per RFC depending on the type of the transport
		parameter, but MUST NOT including the length field unless the parameter is defined so.

		
			( TransportParameter) ID() uint64
			( TransportParameter) Value() []byte
		
			 ActiveConnectionIDLimit
			*DisableActiveMigration
			*FakeQUICTransportParameter
			*GREASEQUICBit
			*GREASETransportParameter
			 InitialMaxData
			 InitialMaxStreamDataBidiLocal
			 InitialMaxStreamDataBidiRemote
			 InitialMaxStreamDataUni
			 InitialMaxStreamsBidi
			 InitialMaxStreamsUni
			 InitialSourceConnectionID
			 MaxAckDelay
			 MaxDatagramFrameSize
			 MaxIdleTimeout
			 MaxUDPPayloadSize
			 PaddingTransportParameter
			*VersionInformation


	

		
			( TransportParameters) Marshal() []byte


	

		
			ClientHelloID ClientHelloID
			Conn *Conn
			Extensions []TLSExtension
			HandshakeState PubClientHandshakeState
			
			
				certCompressionAlgs represents the set of advertised certificate compression
				algorithms, as specified in the ClientHello. This is only relevant client-side, for the
				server certificate. All other forms of certificate compression are unsupported.

			clientHelloBuildStatus ClientHelloBuildStatus
			clientHelloSpec *ClientHelloSpec
			
				activeCall indicates whether Close has been call in the low bit.
				the rest of the bits are the number of goroutines in Conn.Write.

			
				activeCertHandles contains the cache handles to certificates in
				peerCertificates that are used to track active references.

			
				// whether records are buffered in sendBuf

			
				bytesSent counts the bytes of application data sent.
				packetsSent counts packets.

			Conn.cipherSuite uint16
			
				clientFinished and serverFinished contain the Finished message sent
				by the client or server in the most recent handshake. This is
				retained to support the renegotiation extension and tls-unique
				channel-binding.

			
				clientFinishedIsFirst is true if the client sent the first Finished
				message during the most recent handshake. This is recorded because
				the first transmitted Finished message is the tls-unique
				channel-binding value.

			
				clientProtocol is the negotiated ALPN protocol.

			
				closeNotifyErr is any error from sending the alertCloseNotify record.

			
				closeNotifySent is true if the Conn attempted to send an
				alertCloseNotify record.

			
				// configuration passed to constructor

			
				constant

			Conn.curveID CurveID
			
				// whether a HelloRetryRequest was sent/received

			
				// whether this connection was a session resumption

			Conn.echAccepted bool
			
				ekm is a closure for exporting keying material.

			Conn.extMasterSecret bool
			
				// handshake data waiting to be read

			
				// error resulting from handshake

			
				// (*Conn).clientHandshake or serverHandshake

			
				constant after handshake; protected by handshakeMutex

			
				handshakes counts the number of handshakes performed on the
				connection so far. If renegotiation is disabled then this is either
				zero or one.

			
				// version has been negotiated

			
				input/output

			
				// application data waiting to be read, from rawInput.Next

			Conn.isClient bool
			
				isHandshakeComplete is true if the connection is currently transferring
				application data (i.e. is not currently processing a handshake).
				isHandshakeComplete is true implies handshakeErr == nil.

			
				// stapled OCSP response

			
				input/output

			Conn.packetsSent int64
			Conn.peerCertificates []*x509.Certificate
			
				// nil for non-QUIC connections

			
				// raw input, starting with a record header

			
				resumptionSecret is the resumption_master_secret for handling
				or sending NewSessionTicket messages.

			
				retryCount counts the number of consecutive non-advancing records
				received by Conn.readRecord. That is, records that neither advance the
				handshake, nor deliver application data. Protected by in.Mutex.

			
				// signed certificate timestamps from server

			
				secureRenegotiation is true if the server echoed the secure
				renegotiation extension. (This is meaningless as a server because
				renegotiation is not supported in that case.)

			
				// a buffer of records waiting to be sent

			Conn.serverFinished [12]byte
			
				serverName contains the server name indicated by the client, if any.

			
				ticketKeys is the set of active session ticket keys for this
				connection. The first one is used to encrypt new tickets and
				all are tried to decrypt tickets.

			Conn.tmp [16]byte
			
				// [UTLS] used for extensive things such as ALPS, PSK, etc

			
				verifiedChains contains the certificate chains that we built, as
				opposed to the ones presented by the server.

			
				// TLS version

			
				ech extension is a shortcut to the ECH extension in the Extensions slice if there is one.

			
				echCtx is the echContex returned by makeClientHello()

			greaseSeed [5]uint16
			omitSNIExtension bool
			sessionController *sessionController
			
				skipResumptionOnNilExtension is copied from `Config.PreferSkipResumptionOnNilExtension`.
				
				By default, if ClientHelloSpec is predefined or utls-generated (as opposed to HelloCustom), this flag will be updated to true.

		
			(*UConn) ApplyConfig() error
			
				ApplyPreset should only be used in conjunction with HelloCustom to apply custom specs.
				Fields of TLSExtensions that are slices/pointers are shared across different connections with
				same ClientHelloSpec. It is advised to use different specs and avoid any shared state.

			
				BuildHandshakeState behavior varies based on ClientHelloID and
				whether it was already called before.
				If HelloGolang:
				
					[only once] make default ClientHello and overwrite existing state
				
				If any other mimicking ClientHelloID is used:
				
					[only once] make ClientHello based on ID and overwrite existing state
					[each call] apply uconn.Extensions config to internal crypto/tls structures
					[each call] marshal ClientHello.
				
				BuildHandshakeState is automatically called before uTLS performs handshake,
				and should only be called explicitly to inspect/change fields of
				default/mimicked ClientHello.
				With the excpetion of session ticket and psk extensions, which cannot be changed
				after calling BuildHandshakeState, all other fields can be modified.

			
				BuildHandshakeStateWithoutSession is the same as BuildHandshakeState, but does not
				set the session. This is only useful when you want to inspect the ClientHello before
				setting the session manually through SetSessionTicketExtension or SetPSKExtension.
				BuildHandshakeState is automatically called before uTLS performs handshake.

			
				Close closes the connection.

			
				CloseWrite shuts down the writing side of the connection. It should only be
				called once the handshake has completed and does not call CloseWrite on the
				underlying connection. Most callers should just use [Conn.Close].

			
				ConnectionState returns basic TLS details about the connection.

			(*UConn) DidTls12Resume() bool
			
				get current state of cipher and encrypt zeros to get keystream

			(*UConn) GetUnderlyingConn() net.Conn
			
				Handshake runs the client handshake using given clientHandshakeState
				Requires hs.hello, and, optionally, hs.session to be set.

			
				HandshakeContext runs the client or server handshake
				protocol if it has not yet been run.
				
				The provided Context must be non-nil. If the context is canceled before
				the handshake is complete, the handshake is interrupted and an error is returned.
				Once the handshake has completed, cancellation of the context will not affect the
				connection.

			
				LocalAddr returns the local network address.

			(*UConn) MarshalClientHello() error
			
				MarshalClientHelloNoECH marshals ClientHello as if there was no
				ECH extension present.

			
				NetConn returns the underlying connection that is wrapped by c.
				Note that writing to or reading from this connection directly will corrupt the
				TLS session.

			
				OCSPResponse returns the stapled OCSP response from the TLS server, if
				any. (Only valid for client connections.)

			(*UConn) QUICGetTransportParameters() ([]byte, error)
			(*UConn) QUICSetReadSecret(level QUICEncryptionLevel, suite uint16, secret []byte)
			(*UConn) QUICSetWriteSecret(level QUICEncryptionLevel, suite uint16, secret []byte)
			
				Read reads data from the connection.
				
				As Read calls [Conn.Handshake], in order to prevent indefinite blocking a deadline
				must be set for both Read and [Conn.Write] before Read is called when the handshake
				has not yet completed. See [Conn.SetDeadline], [Conn.SetReadDeadline], and
				[Conn.SetWriteDeadline].

			
				RemoteAddr returns the remote network address.

			
				RemoveSNIExtension removes SNI from the list of extensions sent in ClientHello
				It returns an error when used with HelloGolang ClientHelloID

			
				SetClientRandom sets client random explicitly.
				BuildHandshakeFirst() must be called before SetClientRandom.
				r must to be 32 bytes long.

			
				SetDeadline sets the read and write deadlines associated with the connection.
				A zero value for t means [Conn.Read] and [Conn.Write] will not time out.
				After a Write has timed out, the TLS state is corrupt and all future writes will return the same error.

			
				SetPskExtension sets the psk extension for tls 1.3 resumption. This is a no-op if the psk is nil.

			
				SetReadDeadline sets the read deadline on the underlying connection.
				A zero value for t means [Conn.Read] will not time out.

			(*UConn) SetSNI(sni string)
			
				If you want session tickets to be reused - use same cache on following connections

			
				SetSessionState sets the session ticket, which may be preshared or fake.
				If session is nil, the body of session ticket extension will be unset,
				but the extension itself still MAY be present for mimicking purposes.
				Session tickets to be reused - use same cache on following connections.
				
				Deprecated: This method is deprecated in favor of SetSessionTicketExtension,
				as it only handles session override of TLS 1.2

			
				SetSessionTicket sets the session ticket extension.
				If extension is nil, this will be a no-op.

			
				SetTLSVers sets min and max TLS version in all appropriate places.
				Function will use first non-zero version parsed in following order:
				 1. Provided minTLSVers, maxTLSVers
				 2. specExtensions may have SupportedVersionsExtension
				 3. [default] min = TLS 1.0, max = TLS 1.2
				
				Error is only returned if things are in clearly undesirable state
				to help user fix them.

			(*UConn) SetUnderlyingConn(c net.Conn)
			
				SetWriteDeadline sets the write deadline on the underlying connection.
				A zero value for t means [Conn.Write] will not time out.
				After a [Conn.Write] has timed out, the TLS state is corrupt and all future writes will return the same error.

			
				VerifyHostname checks that the peer certificate chain is valid for
				connecting to host. If so, it returns nil; if not, it returns an error
				describing the problem.

			
				Copy-pasted from tls.Conn in its entirety. But c.Handshake() is now utls' one, not tls.
				Write writes data to the connection.

			
			(*UConn) applyPresetByID(id ClientHelloID) (err error)
			(*UConn) buildHandshakeState(loadSession bool) error
			
				clientHandshakeWithOneState checks that exactly one expected state is set (1.2 or 1.3)
				and performs client TLS handshake with that state

			
				clientSessionCacheKey returns a key used to cache sessionTickets that could
				be used to resume previously negotiated TLS sessions with a server.

			( UConn) closeNotify() error
			(*UConn) computeAndUpdateOuterECHExtension(inner *clientHelloMsg, ech *echClientContext, useKey bool) error
			( UConn) connectionStateLocked() ConnectionState
			(*UConn) echTranscriptMsg(outer *clientHelloMsg, echCtx *echClientContext) (err error)
			(*UConn) extensionsList() []uint16
			( UConn) flush() (int, error)
			(*UConn) generateRandomizedSpec() (ClientHelloSpec, error)
			( UConn) getClientCertificate(cri *CertificateRequestInfo) (*Certificate, error)
			( UConn) handleKeyUpdate(keyUpdate *keyUpdateMsg) error
			( UConn) handleNewSessionTicket(msg *newSessionTicketMsgTLS13) error
			
				handlePostHandshakeMessage processes a handshake message arrived after the
				handshake is complete. Up to TLS 1.2, it indicates the start of a renegotiation.

			
				handleRenegotiation processes a HelloRequest handshake message.

			(*UConn) handshakeContext(ctx context.Context) (ret error)
			( UConn) loadSession(hello *clientHelloMsg) (session *SessionState, earlySecret *tls13.EarlySecret, binderKey []byte, err error)
			( UConn) makeClientHello() (*clientHelloMsg, *keySharePrivateKeys, *echClientContext, error)
			( UConn) makeClientHelloForApplyPreset() (*clientHelloMsg, *keySharePrivateKeys, *echClientContext, error)
			
				maxPayloadSizeForWrite returns the maximum TLS payload size to use for the
				next application data record. There is the following trade-off:
				
				  - For latency-sensitive applications, such as web browsing, each TLS
				    record should fit in one TCP segment.
				  - For throughput-sensitive applications, such as large file transfers,
				    larger TLS records better amortize framing and encryption overheads.
				
				A simple heuristic that works well in practice is to use small records for
				the first 1MB of data, then use larger records for subsequent data, and
				reset back to smaller records after the connection becomes idle. See "High
				Performance Web Networking", Chapter 4, or:
				https://www.igvita.com/2013/10/24/optimizing-tls-record-size-and-buffering-latency/
				
				In the interests of simplicity and determinism, this code does not attempt
				to reset the record size once the connection is idle, however.

			( UConn) newRecordHeaderError(conn net.Conn, msg string) (err RecordHeaderError)
			( UConn) pickTLSVersion(serverHello *serverHelloMsg) error
			
				processCertsFromClient takes a chain of client certificates either from a
				Certificates message and verifies them.

			( UConn) processECHClientHello(outer *clientHelloMsg) (*clientHelloMsg, *echServerContext, error)
			( UConn) quicGetTransportParameters() ([]byte, error)
			( UConn) quicHandshakeComplete()
			( UConn) quicReadHandshakeBytes(n int) error
			( UConn) quicRejectedEarlyData()
			( UConn) quicResumeSession(session *SessionState) error
			( UConn) quicSetReadSecret(level QUICEncryptionLevel, suite uint16, secret []byte)
			( UConn) quicSetTransportParameters(params []byte)
			( UConn) quicSetWriteSecret(level QUICEncryptionLevel, suite uint16, secret []byte)
			( UConn) quicStoreSession(session *SessionState)
			
				quicWaitForSignal notifies the QUICConn that handshake progress is blocked,
				and waits for a signal that the handshake should proceed.
				
				The handshake may become blocked waiting for handshake bytes
				or for the user to provide transport parameters.

			( UConn) quicWriteCryptoData(level QUICEncryptionLevel, data []byte)
			( UConn) readChangeCipherSpec() error
			
				readClientHello reads a ClientHello message and selects the protocol version.

			
				readFromUntil reads from r into c.rawInput until c.rawInput contains
				at least n bytes or else returns an error.

			
				readHandshake reads the next handshake message from
				the record layer. If transcript is non-nil, the message
				is written to the passed transcriptHash.

			
				readHandshakeBytes reads handshake data until c.hand contains at least n bytes.

			( UConn) readRecord() error
			
				readRecordOrCCS reads one or more TLS records from the connection and
				updates the record layer state. Some invariants:
				  - c.in must be locked
				  - c.input must be empty
				
				During the handshake one and only one of the following will happen:
				  - c.hand grows
				  - c.in.changeCipherSpec is called
				  - an error is returned
				
				After the handshake one and only one of the following will happen:
				  - c.hand grows
				  - c.input is set
				  - an error is returned

			(*UConn) removeSNIExtension()
			
				retryReadRecord recurs into readRecordOrCCS to drop a non-advancing record, like
				a warning alert, empty application_data, or a change_cipher_spec in TLS 1.3.

			
				sendAlert sends a TLS alert message.

			
				sendAlertLocked sends a TLS alert message.

			( UConn) sendSessionTicket(earlyData bool, extra [][]byte) error
			
				serverHandshake performs a TLS handshake as a server.

			
				sessionState returns a partially filled-out [SessionState] with information
				from the current connection.

			(*UConn) uApplyPatch()
			(*UConn) uLoadSession() error
			( UConn) unmarshalHandshakeMessage(data []byte, transcript transcriptHash) (handshakeMessage, error)
			
				Extending (*Conn).connectionStateLocked()

			
				Extending (*Conn).readHandshake() to support more customized handshake messages.

			
				verifyServerCertificate parses and verifies the provided chain, setting
				c.verifiedChains and c.peerCertificates or sending the appropriate alert.

			( UConn) write(data []byte) (int, error)
			
				writeChangeCipherRecord writes a ChangeCipherSpec message to the connection and
				updates the record layer state.

			
				writeHandshakeRecord writes a handshake message to the connection and updates
				the record layer state. If transcript is non-nil the marshaled message is
				written to it.

			
				writeRecordLocked writes a TLS record with the given type and payload to the
				connection and updates the record layer state.

		
			*UConn : internal/bisect.Writer
			 UConn : io.Closer
			*UConn : io.ReadCloser
			*UConn : io.Reader
			*UConn : io.ReadWriteCloser
			*UConn : io.ReadWriter
			*UConn : io.WriteCloser
			*UConn : io.Writer
			*UConn : net.Conn
			
			*UConn : transcriptHash
			*UConn : crypto/tls.transcriptHash
			 UConn : net/http.closeWriter
		
			func UClient(conn net.Conn, config *Config, clientHelloID ClientHelloID) *UConn
			func (*Roller).Dial(network, addr, serverName string) (*UConn, error)
		
			func ECHExtension.MarshalClientHello(*UConn) error
			func EncryptedClientHelloExtension.MarshalClientHello(*UConn) error
			func (*GREASEEncryptedClientHelloExtension).MarshalClientHello(*UConn) error
			func (*UnimplementedECHExtension).MarshalClientHello(*UConn) error
			
			func newSessionController(uconn *UConn) *sessionController
			func newUQUICConn(uconn *UConn) *UQUICConn
			func (*ALPNExtension).writeToUConn(uc *UConn) error
			func (*CookieExtension).writeToUConn(uc *UConn) error
			func (*ExtendedMasterSecretExtension).writeToUConn(uc *UConn) error
			func (*FakeChannelIDExtension).writeToUConn(uc *UConn) error
			func (*FakeDelegatedCredentialsExtension).writeToUConn(uc *UConn) error
			func (*FakePreSharedKeyExtension).writeToUConn(uc *UConn) error
			func (*FakeRecordSizeLimitExtension).writeToUConn(uc *UConn) error
			func (*FakeTokenBindingExtension).writeToUConn(uc *UConn) error
			func (*GenericExtension).writeToUConn(uc *UConn) error
			func (*GREASEEncryptedClientHelloExtension).writeToUConn(uconn *UConn) error
			func (*KeyShareExtension).writeToUConn(uc *UConn) error
			func (*NPNExtension).writeToUConn(uc *UConn) error
			func (*PSKKeyExchangeModesExtension).writeToUConn(uc *UConn) error
			func (*QUICTransportParametersExtension).writeToUConn(*UConn) error
			func (*RenegotiationInfoExtension).writeToUConn(uc *UConn) error
			func (*SCTExtension).writeToUConn(uc *UConn) error
			func (*SessionTicketExtension).writeToUConn(uc *UConn) error
			func (*SignatureAlgorithmsCertExtension).writeToUConn(uc *UConn) error
			func (*SignatureAlgorithmsExtension).writeToUConn(uc *UConn) error
			func (*SNIExtension).writeToUConn(uc *UConn) error
			func (*StatusRequestExtension).writeToUConn(uc *UConn) error
			func (*StatusRequestV2Extension).writeToUConn(uc *UConn) error
			func (*SupportedCurvesExtension).writeToUConn(uc *UConn) error
			func (*SupportedPointsExtension).writeToUConn(uc *UConn) error
			func (*SupportedVersionsExtension).writeToUConn(uc *UConn) error
			func (*UnimplementedECHExtension).writeToUConn(_ *UConn) error
			func (*UnimplementedPreSharedKeyExtension).writeToUConn(*UConn) error
			func (*UtlsCompressCertExtension).writeToUConn(uc *UConn) error
			func (*UtlsGREASEExtension).writeToUConn(uc *UConn) error
			func (*UtlsPaddingExtension).writeToUConn(uc *UConn) error
			func (*UtlsPreSharedKeyExtension).writeToUConn(uc *UConn) error


	
		UnimplementedECHExtension is a placeholder for an ECH extension that is not implemented.
		All implementations of EncryptedClientHelloExtension should embed this struct to ensure
		forward compatibility.

		
			
				Len implements TLSExtension.

			
				MarshalClientHello implements EncryptedClientHelloExtension.

			
				Read implements TLSExtension.

			
			
				mustEmbedUnimplementedECHExtension is a noop function but is required to
				ensure forward compatibility.

			
				writeToUConn implements TLSExtension.

		
			*UnimplementedECHExtension : EncryptedClientHelloExtension
			*UnimplementedECHExtension : TLSExtension
			*UnimplementedECHExtension : io.Reader


	

		
			(*UnimplementedPreSharedKeyExtension) GetPreSharedKeyCommon() PreSharedKeyCommon
			(*UnimplementedPreSharedKeyExtension) InitializeByUtls(session *SessionState, earlySecret []byte, binderKey []byte, identities []PskIdentity)
			(*UnimplementedPreSharedKeyExtension) IsInitialized() bool
			(*UnimplementedPreSharedKeyExtension) Len() int
			(*UnimplementedPreSharedKeyExtension) PatchBuiltHello(hello *PubClientHelloMsg) error
			(*UnimplementedPreSharedKeyExtension) Read([]byte) (int, error)
			(*UnimplementedPreSharedKeyExtension) SetOmitEmptyPsk(val bool)
			
			( UnimplementedPreSharedKeyExtension) mustEmbedUnimplementedPreSharedKeyExtension()
			(*UnimplementedPreSharedKeyExtension) writeToUConn(*UConn) error
		
			*UnimplementedPreSharedKeyExtension : Initializable
			*UnimplementedPreSharedKeyExtension : PreSharedKeyExtension
			*UnimplementedPreSharedKeyExtension : TLSExtension
			*UnimplementedPreSharedKeyExtension : io.Reader


	
		A UQUICConn represents a connection which uses a QUIC implementation as the underlying
		transport as described in RFC 9001.
		
		Methods of UQUICConn are not safe for concurrent use.

		
			
			conn *UConn
			sessionTicketSent bool
		
			(*UQUICConn) ApplyPreset(p *ClientHelloSpec) error
			
				Close closes the connection and stops any in-progress handshake.

			
				ConnectionState returns basic TLS details about the connection.

			
				HandleData handles handshake bytes received from the peer.
				It may produce connection events, which may be read with NextEvent.

			
				NextEvent returns the next event occurring on the connection.
				It returns an event with a Kind of QUICNoEvent when no events are available.

			
				SendSessionTicket sends a session ticket to the client.
				It produces connection events, which may be read with NextEvent.
				Currently, it can only be called once.

			
				SetTransportParameters sets the transport parameters to send to the peer.
				
				Server connections may delay setting the transport parameters until after
				receiving the client's transport parameters. See QUICTransportParametersRequired.

			
				Start starts the client or server handshake protocol.
				It may produce connection events, which may be read with NextEvent.
				
				Start must be called at most once.

		
			*UQUICConn : io.Closer
		
			func UQUICClient(config *QUICConfig, clientHelloID ClientHelloID) *UQUICConn
			
			func newUQUICConn(uconn *UConn) *UQUICConn

		(https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7250) are not supported.
		
		See https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc8879#section-3

		activeCert is a handle to a certificate held in the cache. Once there are
		no alive activeCerts for a given certificate, the certificate is removed
		from the cache by a finalizer.

		
			
			cert *x509.Certificate


	

		
			
				NonceSize returns the size of the nonce that must be passed to Seal
				and Open.

			
				Open decrypts and authenticates ciphertext, authenticates the
				additional data and, if successful, appends the resulting plaintext
				to dst, returning the updated slice. The nonce must be NonceSize()
				bytes long and both it and the additional data must match the
				value passed to Seal.
				
				To reuse ciphertext's storage for the decrypted output, use ciphertext[:0]
				as dst. Otherwise, the remaining capacity of dst must not overlap ciphertext.
				dst and additionalData may not overlap.
				
				Even if the function fails, the contents of dst, up to its capacity,
				may be overwritten.

			
				Overhead returns the maximum difference between the lengths of a
				plaintext and its ciphertext.

			
				Seal encrypts and authenticates plaintext, authenticates the
				additional data and appends the result to dst, returning the updated
				slice. The nonce must be NonceSize() bytes long and unique for all
				time, for a given key.
				
				To reuse plaintext's storage for the encrypted output, use plaintext[:0]
				as dst. Otherwise, the remaining capacity of dst must not overlap plaintext.
				dst and additionalData may not overlap.

			
			
				explicitNonceLen returns the number of bytes of explicit nonce
				included in each record. This is eight for older AEADs and
				zero for modern ones.

		
			
			*prefixNonceAEAD
			*xorNonceAEAD
		
			 aead : crypto/cipher.AEAD
		
			
			func aeadAESGCM(key, noncePrefix []byte) aead
			func aeadAESGCMTLS13(key, nonceMask []byte) aead
			func aeadChaCha20Poly1305(key, nonceMask []byte) aead

			( alert) String() string
		
			 alert : error
			 alert : fmt.Stringer
			
			 alert : context.stringer
			 alert : runtime.stringer
		
			
			func (*Conn).sendAlert(err alert) error
			func (*Conn).sendAlertLocked(err alert) error
		
			
			const alertAccessDenied
			const alertBadCertificate
			const alertBadCertificateHashValue
			const alertBadCertificateStatusResponse
			const alertBadRecordMAC
			const alertCertificateExpired
			const alertCertificateRequired
			const alertCertificateRevoked
			const alertCertificateUnknown
			const alertCertificateUnobtainable
			const alertCloseNotify
			const alertDecodeError
			const alertDecompressionFailure
			const alertDecryptError
			const alertDecryptionFailed
			const alertECHRequired
			const alertExportRestriction
			const alertHandshakeFailure
			const alertIllegalParameter

			const alertInsufficientSecurity

			const alertUnexpectedMessage

			const alertUnrecognizedName
			const alertUnsupportedCertificate
			const alertUnsupportedExtension
			const alertUserCanceled

		atLeastReader reads from R, stopping with EOF once at least N bytes have been
		read. It is different from an io.LimitedReader in that it doesn't cut short
		the last Read call, and in that it considers an early EOF an error.

		
			
				// max bytes remaining

			
				// underlying reader

		
			(*atLeastReader) Read(p []byte) (int, error)
		
			*atLeastReader : io.Reader


	

		
			
			cert *x509.Certificate
			refs atomic.Int64


	
		cbcMode is an interface for block ciphers using cipher block chaining.

		
			
				BlockSize returns the mode's block size.

			
				CryptBlocks encrypts or decrypts a number of blocks. The length of
				src must be a multiple of the block size. Dst and src must overlap
				entirely or not at all.
				
				If len(dst) < len(src), CryptBlocks should panic. It is acceptable
				to pass a dst bigger than src, and in that case, CryptBlocks will
				only update dst[:len(src)] and will not touch the rest of dst.
				
				Multiple calls to CryptBlocks behave as if the concatenation of
				the src buffers was passed in a single run. That is, BlockMode
				maintains state and does not reset at each CryptBlocks call.

			( cbcMode) SetIV([]byte)
		
			*crypto/internal/fips140/aes.CBCDecrypter
			*crypto/internal/fips140/aes.CBCEncrypter
			
			*crypto/cipher.cbcDecrypter
			*crypto/cipher.cbcEncrypter
			 crypto/tls.cbcMode (interface)
		
			 cbcMode : github.com/gotd/ige.IGE
			 cbcMode : crypto/cipher.BlockMode
			
			 cbcMode : crypto/tls.cbcMode


	
		certCache implements an intern table for reference counted x509.Certificates,
		implemented in a similar fashion to BoringSSL's CRYPTO_BUFFER_POOL. This
		allows for a single x509.Certificate to be kept in memory and referenced from
		multiple Conns. Returned references should not be mutated by callers. Certificates
		are still safe to use after they are removed from the cache.
		
		Certificates are returned wrapped in an activeCert struct that should be held by
		the caller. When references to the activeCert are freed, the number of references
		to the certificate in the cache is decremented. Once the number of references
		reaches zero, the entry is evicted from the cache.
		
		The main difference between this implementation and CRYPTO_BUFFER_POOL is that
		CRYPTO_BUFFER_POOL is a more  generic structure which supports blobs of data,
		rather than specific structures. Since we only care about x509.Certificates,
		certCache is implemented as a specific cache, rather than a generic one.
		
		See https://boringssl.googlesource.com/boringssl/+/master/include/openssl/pool.h
		and https://boringssl.googlesource.com/boringssl/+/master/crypto/pool/pool.c
		for the BoringSSL reference.

		
			Map sync.Map
			
			Map.m isync.HashTrieMap[any, any]
		
			
				Clear deletes all the entries, resulting in an empty Map.

			
				CompareAndDelete deletes the entry for key if its value is equal to old.
				The old value must be of a comparable type.
				
				If there is no current value for key in the map, CompareAndDelete
				returns false (even if the old value is the nil interface value).

			
				CompareAndSwap swaps the old and new values for key
				if the value stored in the map is equal to old.
				The old value must be of a comparable type.

			
				Delete deletes the value for a key.
				If the key is not in the map, Delete does nothing.

			
				Load returns the value stored in the map for a key, or nil if no
				value is present.
				The ok result indicates whether value was found in the map.

			
				LoadAndDelete deletes the value for a key, returning the previous value if any.
				The loaded result reports whether the key was present.

			
				LoadOrStore returns the existing value for the key if present.
				Otherwise, it stores and returns the given value.
				The loaded result is true if the value was loaded, false if stored.

			
				Range calls f sequentially for each key and value present in the map.
				If f returns false, range stops the iteration.
				
				Range does not necessarily correspond to any consistent snapshot of the Map's
				contents: no key will be visited more than once, but if the value for any key
				is stored or deleted concurrently (including by f), Range may reflect any
				mapping for that key from any point during the Range call. Range does not
				block other methods on the receiver; even f itself may call any method on m.
				
				Range may be O(N) with the number of elements in the map even if f returns
				false after a constant number of calls.

			
				Store sets the value for a key.

			
				Swap swaps the value for a key and returns the previous value if any.
				The loaded result reports whether the key was present.

			
			
				active increments the number of references to the entry, wraps the
				certificate in the entry in an activeCert, and sets the finalizer.
				
				Note that there is a race between active and the finalizer set on the
				returned activeCert, triggered if active is called after the ref count is
				decremented such that refs may be > 0 when evict is called. We consider this
				safe, since the caller holding an activeCert for an entry that is no longer
				in the cache is fine, with the only side effect being the memory overhead of
				there being more than one distinct reference to a certificate alive at once.

			
				evict removes a cacheEntry from the cache.

			
				newCert returns a x509.Certificate parsed from der. If there is already a copy
				of the certificate in the cache, a reference to the existing certificate will
				be returned. Otherwise, a fresh certificate will be added to the cache, and
				the reference returned. The returned reference should not be mutated.

		
			
			  var globalCertCache *certCache


	

		
			
			certificates [][]byte
		
			
			(*certificateMsg) marshal() ([]byte, error)
			(*certificateMsg) unmarshal(data []byte) bool
		
			
			*certificateMsg : handshakeMessage


	

		
			
			certificate Certificate
			ocspStapling bool
			scts bool
		
			
			(*certificateMsgTLS13) marshal() ([]byte, error)
			(*certificateMsgTLS13) unmarshal(data []byte) bool

			certificateAuthorities [][]byte
			certificateTypes []byte

				supported signature algorithms. This change was introduced with TLS 1.2.

			supportedSignatureAlgorithms []SignatureScheme
		
			
			(*certificateRequestMsg) marshal() ([]byte, error)

			*certificateRequestMsg : handshakeMessage

			certificateAuthorities [][]byte
			ocspStapling bool
			
				// [uTLS]

			scts bool
			supportedSignatureAlgorithms []SignatureScheme
			supportedSignatureAlgorithmsCert []SignatureScheme
		
			
			(*certificateRequestMsgTLS13) marshal() ([]byte, error)
			
				[UTLS SECTION BEGINS]

			(*certificateRequestMsgTLS13) toPublic() *CertificateRequestMsgTLS13

			*certificateRequestMsgTLS13 : handshakeMessageWithOriginalBytes
		
			
			func (*CertificateRequestMsgTLS13).toPrivate() *certificateRequestMsgTLS13


	

		
			
			response []byte
		
			
			(*certificateStatusMsg) marshal() ([]byte, error)
			(*certificateStatusMsg) unmarshal(data []byte) bool
		
			
			*certificateStatusMsg : handshakeMessage


	

		
			
			
				// format change introduced in TLS 1.2

			signature []byte

			*certificateVerifyMsg : handshakeMessage


	
		A cipherSuite is a TLS 1.0–1.2 cipher suite, and defines the key exchange
		mechanism, as well as the cipher+MAC pair or the AEAD.

		
			
			aead func(key, fixedNonce []byte) aead
			cipher func(key, iv []byte, isRead bool) any
			
				flags is a bitmask of the suite* values, above.

			id uint16
			ivLen int
			ka func(version uint16) keyAgreement
			
				the lengths, in bytes, of the key material needed for each component.

			mac func(key []byte) hash.Hash
			macLen int
		
			
			(*cipherSuite) getPublicObj() PubCipherSuite
		
			
			func cipherSuiteByID(id uint16) *cipherSuite
			func mutualCipherSuite(have []uint16, want uint16) *cipherSuite
			func selectCipherSuite(ids, supportedIDs []uint16, ok func(*cipherSuite) bool) *cipherSuite
			func (*PubCipherSuite).getPrivatePtr() *cipherSuite
		
			
			func ekmFromMasterSecret(version uint16, suite *cipherSuite, masterSecret, clientRandom, serverRandom []byte) func(string, []byte, int) ([]byte, error)
			func extMasterFromPreMasterSecret(version uint16, suite *cipherSuite, preMasterSecret, transcript []byte) []byte
			func keysFromMasterSecret(version uint16, suite *cipherSuite, masterSecret, clientRandom, serverRandom []byte, macLen, keyLen, ivLen int) (clientMAC, serverMAC, clientKey, serverKey, clientIV, serverIV []byte)
			func masterFromPreMasterSecret(version uint16, suite *cipherSuite, preMasterSecret, clientRandom, serverRandom []byte) []byte
			func newFinishedHash(version uint16, cipherSuite *cipherSuite) finishedHash
			func prfAndHashForVersion(version uint16, suite *cipherSuite) (prfFunc, crypto.Hash)
			func prfForVersion(version uint16, suite *cipherSuite) prfFunc


	
		A cipherSuiteTLS13 defines only the pair of the AEAD algorithm and hash
		algorithm to be used with HKDF. See RFC 8446, Appendix B.4.

		
			
			aead func(key, fixedNonce []byte) aead
			hash crypto.Hash
			id uint16
			keyLen int
		
			
			
				exportKeyingMaterial implements RFC5705 exporters for TLS 1.3 according to
				RFC 8446, Section 7.5.

			
				finishedHash generates the Finished verify_data or PskBinderEntry according
				to RFC 8446, Section 4.4.4. See sections 4.4 and 4.2.11.2 for the baseKey
				selection.

			
				nextTrafficSecret generates the next traffic secret, given the current one,
				according to RFC 8446, Section 7.2.

			(*cipherSuiteTLS13) toPublic() *PubCipherSuiteTLS13
			
				trafficKey generates traffic keys according to RFC 8446, Section 7.3.

		
			
			func cipherSuiteTLS13ByID(id uint16) *cipherSuiteTLS13
			func mutualCipherSuiteTLS13(have []uint16, want uint16) *cipherSuiteTLS13
			func (*PubCipherSuiteTLS13).toPrivate() *cipherSuiteTLS13


	

		
			
			c *Conn
			ctx context.Context
			finishedHash finishedHash
			hello *clientHelloMsg
			masterSecret []byte
			serverHello *serverHelloMsg
			
				// the session being resumed

			suite *cipherSuite
			
				// a fresh ticket received during this handshake

			
				// [uTLS]

		
			
			(*clientHandshakeState) doFullHandshake() error
			(*clientHandshakeState) establishKeys() error
			
				Does the handshake, either a full one or resumes old session. Requires hs.c,
				hs.hello, hs.serverHello, and, optionally, hs.session to be set.

			(*clientHandshakeState) pickCipherSuite() error
			(*clientHandshakeState) processServerHello() (bool, error)
			(*clientHandshakeState) readFinished(out []byte) error
			(*clientHandshakeState) readSessionTicket() error
			(*clientHandshakeState) saveSessionTicket() error
			(*clientHandshakeState) sendFinished(out []byte) error
			(*clientHandshakeState) serverResumedSession() bool
			(*clientHandshakeState) toPublic12() *PubClientHandshakeState
		
			
			func (*PubClientHandshakeState).toPrivate12() *clientHandshakeState


	

		
			
			binderKey []byte
			c *Conn
			certReq *certificateRequestMsgTLS13
			ctx context.Context
			earlySecret *tls13.EarlySecret
			echContext *echClientContext
			hello *clientHelloMsg
			keyShareKeys *keySharePrivateKeys
			masterSecret *tls13.MasterSecret
			sentDummyCCS bool
			serverHello *serverHelloMsg
			session *SessionState
			suite *cipherSuiteTLS13
			
				// client_application_traffic_secret_0

			transcript hash.Hash
			
				// [uTLS]

			usingPSK bool
		
			
			
				checkServerHelloOrHRR does validity checks that apply to both ServerHello and
				HelloRetryRequest messages. It sets hs.suite.

			
				called by (*clientHandshakeStateTLS13).utlsReadServerCertificate() when UtlsCompressCertExtension is used

			(*clientHandshakeStateTLS13) establishHandshakeKeys() error
			
				handshake requires hs.c, hs.hello, hs.serverHello, hs.keyShareKeys, and,
				optionally, hs.session, hs.earlySecret and hs.binderKey to be set.

			
				processHelloRetryRequest handles the HRR in hs.serverHello, modifies and
				resends hs.hello, and reads the new ServerHello into hs.serverHello.

			(*clientHandshakeStateTLS13) processServerHello() error
			(*clientHandshakeStateTLS13) readServerCertificate() error
			(*clientHandshakeStateTLS13) readServerFinished() error
			(*clientHandshakeStateTLS13) readServerParameters() error

			(*clientHandshakeStateTLS13) sendClientFinished() error
			
				sendDummyChangeCipherSpec sends a ChangeCipherSpec record for compatibility
				with middleboxes that didn't implement TLS correctly. See RFC 8446, Appendix D.4.

			
				to be called in (*clientHandshakeStateTLS13).handshake(),
				after hs.readServerFinished() and before hs.sendClientCertificate()

			(*clientHandshakeStateTLS13) toPublic13() *PubClientHandshakeState
			
				This function is called by (*clientHandshakeStateTLS13).readServerCertificate()
				to retrieve the certificate out of a message read by (*Conn).readHandshake()

				marshalWithoutBinders returns the ClientHello through the
				PreSharedKeyExtension.identities field, according to RFC 8446, Section
				4.2.11.2. Note that m.pskBinders must be set to slices of the correct length.

			(*clientHelloMsg) originalBytes() []byte
			(*clientHelloMsg) unmarshal(data []byte) bool
			
				updateBinders updates the m.pskBinders field. The supplied binders must have
				the same length as the current m.pskBinders.

				BlockSize returns the hash's underlying block size.
				The Write method must be able to accept any amount
				of data, but it may operate more efficiently if all writes
				are a multiple of the block size.

			( constantTimeHash) ConstantTimeSum(b []byte) []byte
			
				Reset resets the Hash to its initial state.

			
				Size returns the number of bytes Sum will return.

			
				Sum appends the current hash to b and returns the resulting slice.
				It does not change the underlying hash state.

			( constantTimeHash) Write([]byte) (int, error)

		ecdheKeyAgreement implements a TLS key agreement where the server
		generates an ephemeral EC public/private key pair and signs it. The
		pre-master secret is then calculated using ECDH. The signature may
		be ECDSA, Ed25519 or RSA.

		
			
			
				ckx and preMasterSecret are generated in processServerKeyExchange
				and returned in generateClientKeyExchange.

			key *ecdh.PrivateKey
			preMasterSecret []byte

			(*ecdheKeyAgreement) processServerKeyExchange(config *Config, clientHello *clientHelloMsg, serverHello *serverHelloMsg, cert *x509.Certificate, skx *serverKeyExchangeMsg) error

		A finishedHash calculates the hash of a set of handshake messages suitable
		for including in a Finished message.

		
			
			
				In TLS 1.2, a full buffer is sadly required.

			client hash.Hash
			
				Prior to TLS 1.2, an additional MD5 hash is required.

			prf prfFunc
			server hash.Hash
			serverMD5 hash.Hash
			version uint16
		
			( finishedHash) Sum() []byte
			(*finishedHash) Write(msg []byte) (n int, err error)
			
			
				clientSum returns the contents of the verify_data member of a client's
				Finished message.

			
				discardHandshakeBuffer is called when there is no more need to
				buffer the entirety of the handshake messages.

			(*finishedHash) getPublicObj() FinishedHash
			
				hashForClientCertificate returns the handshake messages so far, pre-hashed if
				necessary, suitable for signing by a TLS client certificate.

			
				serverSum returns the contents of the verify_data member of a server's
				Finished message.

		
			*finishedHash : internal/bisect.Writer
			*finishedHash : io.Writer

			*finishedHash : crypto/tls.transcriptHash

		A halfConn represents one direction of the record layer
		connection, either sending or receiving.

		
			Mutex sync.Mutex
			
			
				// cipher algorithm

			
				// first permanent error

			
				// current QUIC encryption level

			mac hash.Hash
			Mutex.mu isync.Mutex
			
				// next encryption state

			
				// next MAC algorithm

			
				// to avoid allocs; interface method args escape

			
				// 64-bit sequence number

			
				// current TLS 1.3 traffic secret

			
				// protocol version

		
			
				Lock locks m.
				If the lock is already in use, the calling goroutine
				blocks until the mutex is available.

			
				TryLock tries to lock m and reports whether it succeeded.
				
				Note that while correct uses of TryLock do exist, they are rare,
				and use of TryLock is often a sign of a deeper problem
				in a particular use of mutexes.

			
				Unlock unlocks m.
				It is a run-time error if m is not locked on entry to Unlock.
				
				A locked [Mutex] is not associated with a particular goroutine.
				It is allowed for one goroutine to lock a Mutex and then
				arrange for another goroutine to unlock it.

			
			
				changeCipherSpec changes the encryption and MAC states
				to the ones previously passed to prepareCipherSpec.

			
				decrypt authenticates and decrypts the record if protection is active at
				this stage. The returned plaintext might overlap with the input.

			
				encrypt encrypts payload, adding the appropriate nonce and/or MAC, and
				appends it to record, which must already contain the record header.

			
				explicitNonceLen returns the number of bytes of explicit nonce or IV included
				in each record. Explicit nonces are present only in CBC modes after TLS 1.0
				and in certain AEAD modes in TLS 1.2.

			
				incSeq increments the sequence number.

			
				prepareCipherSpec sets the encryption and MAC states
				that a subsequent changeCipherSpec will use.

			(*halfConn) setErrorLocked(err error) error
			(*halfConn) setTrafficSecret(suite *cipherSuiteTLS13, level QUICEncryptionLevel, secret []byte)
		
			*halfConn : sync.Locker

			( handshakeMessage) unmarshal([]byte) bool

			*encryptedExtensionsMsg
			*endOfEarlyDataMsg
			*finishedMsg
			 handshakeMessageWithOriginalBytes (interface)

			*newSessionTicketMsgTLS13
			*serverHelloDoneMsg
			*serverHelloMsg
			*serverKeyExchangeMsg

			*utlsCompressedCertificateMsg

			( handshakeMessageWithOriginalBytes) marshal() ([]byte, error)
			
				originalBytes should return the original bytes that were passed to
				unmarshal to create the message. If the message was not produced by
				unmarshal, it should return nil.

			( handshakeMessageWithOriginalBytes) unmarshal([]byte) bool
		
			
			*certificateRequestMsgTLS13

		A keyAgreement implements the client and server side of a TLS 1.0–1.2 key

				In the case that the key agreement protocol doesn't use a

				nil.

			( keyAgreement) processClientKeyExchange(*Config, *Certificate, *clientKeyExchangeMsg, uint16) ([]byte, error)
			
				This method may not be called if the server doesn't send a
				ServerKeyExchange message.

		A listener implements a network listener (net.Listener) for TLS connections.

		
			Listener net.Listener
			
			config *Config
		
			
				Accept waits for and returns the next incoming TLS connection.
				The returned connection is of type *Conn.

			
				Addr returns the listener's network address.

			
				Close closes the listener.
				Any blocked Accept operations will be unblocked and return errors.

		
			 listener : io.Closer
			*listener : net.Listener


	
		lruSessionCache is a ClientSessionCache implementation that uses an LRU
		caching strategy.

		
			Mutex sync.Mutex
			
			capacity int
			m map[string]*list.Element
			Mutex.mu isync.Mutex
			q *list.List
		
			
				Get returns the [ClientSessionState] value associated with a given key. It
				returns (nil, false) if no value is found.

			
				Lock locks m.
				If the lock is already in use, the calling goroutine
				blocks until the mutex is available.

			
				Put adds the provided (sessionKey, cs) pair to the cache. If cs is nil, the entry
				corresponding to sessionKey is removed from the cache instead.

			
				TryLock tries to lock m and reports whether it succeeded.
				
				Note that while correct uses of TryLock do exist, they are rare,
				and use of TryLock is often a sign of a deeper problem
				in a particular use of mutexes.

			
				Unlock unlocks m.
				It is a run-time error if m is not locked on entry to Unlock.
				
				A locked [Mutex] is not associated with a particular goroutine.
				It is allowed for one goroutine to lock a Mutex and then
				arrange for another goroutine to unlock it.

		
			*lruSessionCache : ClientSessionCache
			*lruSessionCache : sync.Locker


	

		
			
			sessionKey string
			state *ClientSessionState

		prefixNonceAEAD wraps an AEAD and prefixes a fixed portion of the nonce to
